# usermgr

```go
import "github.com/xieyiminglc/openapi-docs-demo/go-sdk-demo/usermgr"
```

Package usermgr provides a small in\-memory client for managing users.

It exists as a documentation demo for the openapi\-docs\-demo project, paired with the equivalent HTTP services in go\-server/, python\-server/, and ts\-server/.

### Quick start

```
client := usermgr.New()
id, _ := client.Create(usermgr.CreateParams{
    Name:  "alice",
    Age:   20,
    Email: "alice@example.com",
})
user, _ := client.Get(id)
fmt.Println(user.Name)
```

### Error handling

Lookups for missing IDs return [ErrNotFound](<#ErrNotFound>); validation errors return [\\\*ValidationError](<#ValidationError>) so callers can distinguish them with [errors.Is](<https://pkg.go.dev/errors/#Is>) and [errors.As](<https://pkg.go.dev/errors/#As>).

## Variables

<a name="ErrNotFound"></a>ErrNotFound is returned by [Client.Get](<#Client.Get>) when no user matches the given ID.

```go
var ErrNotFound = errors.New("usermgr: user not found")
```

<a name="Client"></a>
## type Client

Client is a thread\-safe handle to an in\-memory user store.

The zero value is not usable; obtain a Client via [New](<#New>).

```go
type Client struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func New

```go
func New() *Client
```

New returns a ready\-to\-use [Client](<#Client>) with an empty store.

<a name="Client.Create"></a>
### func \(\*Client\) Create

```go
func (c *Client) Create(p CreateParams) (int64, error)
```

Create validates p and inserts a new user, returning its generated ID.

Validation rules:

- Name must be 1–31 characters
- Age must be in \[0, 200\)
- Email must contain "@"

On any validation failure Create returns a [\\\*ValidationError](<#ValidationError>) describing the first offending field.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/xieyiminglc/openapi-docs-demo/go-sdk-demo/usermgr"
)

func main() {
	c := usermgr.New()
	id, err := c.Create(usermgr.CreateParams{
		Name:  "alice",
		Age:   20,
		Email: "alice@example.com",
	})
	fmt.Println(id, err)
}
```

#### Output

```
1 <nil>
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Client.Get"></a>
### func \(\*Client\) Get

```go
func (c *Client) Get(id int64) (User, error)
```

Get returns the user with the given ID.

It returns [ErrNotFound](<#ErrNotFound>) \(wrappable with [errors.Is](<https://pkg.go.dev/errors/#Is>)\) if no such user exists.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/xieyiminglc/openapi-docs-demo/go-sdk-demo/usermgr"
)

func main() {
	c := usermgr.New()
	id, _ := c.Create(usermgr.CreateParams{Name: "bob", Age: 30, Email: "b@e.com"})

	u, _ := c.Get(id)
	fmt.Println(u.Name)
}
```

#### Output

```
bob
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Not Found)</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/xieyiminglc/openapi-docs-demo/go-sdk-demo/usermgr"
)

func main() {
	c := usermgr.New()
	_, err := c.Get(404)
	fmt.Println(errors.Is(err, usermgr.ErrNotFound))
}
```

#### Output

```
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Client.List"></a>
### func \(\*Client\) List

```go
func (c *Client) List(opts ListOptions) (users []User, total int)
```

List returns users matching opts plus the total count \*before\* pagination.

Results are sorted by ID ascending for deterministic output.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/xieyiminglc/openapi-docs-demo/go-sdk-demo/usermgr"
)

func main() {
	c := usermgr.New()
	_, _ = c.Create(usermgr.CreateParams{Name: "alice", Age: 20, Email: "a@e.com"})
	_, _ = c.Create(usermgr.CreateParams{Name: "bob", Age: 30, Email: "b@e.com"})
	_, _ = c.Create(usermgr.CreateParams{Name: "carol", Age: 40, Email: "c@e.com"})

	page, total := c.List(usermgr.ListOptions{Keyword: "a", Page: 1, Size: 10})
	fmt.Println(total, len(page), page[0].Name, page[1].Name)
}
```

#### Output

```
2 2 alice carol
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="CreateParams"></a>
## type CreateParams

CreateParams carries the fields needed to register a new user.

All fields are required. See [Client.Create](<#Client.Create>) for the validation rules.

```go
type CreateParams struct {
    Name  string
    Age   int
    Email string
}
```

<a name="ListOptions"></a>
## type ListOptions

ListOptions filters and paginates [Client.List](<#Client.List>) results.

A zero\-value ListOptions returns all users in insertion order.

```go
type ListOptions struct {
    // Keyword filters users whose Name contains this substring (case-insensitive).
    Keyword string
    // Page is 1-based. Zero is treated as 1.
    Page int
    // Size caps results per page. Zero is treated as 20. Maximum is 100.
    Size int
}
```

<a name="User"></a>
## type User

User is the canonical user record returned by the client.

```go
type User struct {
    ID    int64
    Name  string
    Age   int
    Email string
}
```

<a name="ValidationError"></a>
## type ValidationError

ValidationError describes one or more invalid fields on the input.

Callers can match this with [errors.As](<https://pkg.go.dev/errors/#As>) to surface field\-level details to end users.

```go
type ValidationError struct {
    Field   string
    Message string
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/xieyiminglc/openapi-docs-demo/go-sdk-demo/usermgr"
)

func main() {
	c := usermgr.New()
	_, err := c.Create(usermgr.CreateParams{Name: "", Age: 20, Email: "x@y.com"})

	var ve *usermgr.ValidationError
	if errors.As(err, &ve) {
		fmt.Println(ve.Field, "->", ve.Message)
	}
}
```

#### Output

```
Name -> must be 1-31 chars
```

</p>
</details>

